summary: metrics-pipeline-d8k2 ships postinstall beacons that exfiltrate host info over HTTP
details: The package declares a postinstall hook (`"postinstall": "node run.js"`) that runs automatically on `npm install`. The tarball contains beacon scripts (`beacon34.js`, `beacon_linux.js`) which combine `child_process`, `os`, `http`/`https`, `os.hostname()`, `os.platform()`, and `process.env` reads with outbound `http.get` / `http.request` POST and GET calls. The composition — system identity collection plus child_process plus outbound HTTP at install time — is the canonical install-time host-beacon / exfiltration shape, with no library functionality that would justify it. Installing the package on a developer or CI machine causes it to fingerprint the host (platform, hostname, environment variables) and report to a remote endpoint, and the `child_process` import in the same script gives the operator a follow-on command-execution primitive.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
